steps.title: Make prior action a modifier
steps.content: The sentence needs "Udé" as the subject and "honors" as the main verb. The material about referring to Nollywood as "Africa's vivid mirror par excellence" should function as a modifying phrase between the subject and main verb.

steps.title: Reject extra finite verbs
steps.content: Choices A, B, and C all create a second finite verb before "honors," leaving two main verbs for the same subject without proper coordination. The remaining choice turns the prior action into a participial modifier, so the sentence stays grammatical. The answer is D.
